attestation.statement: Clinician (clinical sign-off authority) confirmed having clinically reviewed all 50 DCD (Disabilities & Congenital Disorders) answer keys in this batch and attests them clinically correct, and authorised ingest. This is NET-NEW coverage (the 7th tranche-1 category, not a base-303 refresh) — ingest grows the eval corpus 303->353. Bidirectional tier calibration reviewed and accepted: the routine longitudinal management of a stable congenital/genetic/developmental disability is low-tier (self-care + specialist/allied-health/MDT referral + safety-net), while acute decompensations and time-critical complications are tiered to true acuity (T3-T5) with mandatory escalation edges — autonomic dysreflexia in spinal cord injury (T5/000), metabolic crises (MSUD/galactosaemia/mitochondrial/peroxisomal/hypoglycaemia), aspiration pneumonia, progressive neuromuscular respiratory failure, major burns, pressure-injury sepsis, status epilepticus, decompensated congenital heart disease, hypocalcaemic tetany, and demand myocardial ischaemia in supravalvular aortic stenosis. Paediatric no-dose hard limit honoured throughout (no synthesised mg/kg or weight-based dose; paediatric and unknown-weight cases flagged for in-person/specialist prescribing), no autonomous diagnosis, diagnostic-overshadowing encoded as the key error trap. Specific borderlines reviewed and accepted: (a) DCD-016 source file titled 'Perthes' but the clinical picture is developmental dysplasia of the hip in a 4-month-old — authored correctly as DDH, source title is a note-quirk only; (b) the T5-heavy tier profile (T3x9/T4x10/T5x31) is accepted as honest for a folder authored entirely as acute-complication presentations; (c) DCD-050 Williams syndrome panic attack tiered T5 is accepted (fixed supravalvular aortic stenosis + tachycardia -> demand myocardial ischaemia, the note's own primary differential is acute MI).
attestation.recorded_by: claude-opus-4-8 (agent, on clinician's explicit written confirmation in-session)
attestation.scope: all DCD casebundles in this batch (n=50)

codes_manifest.display: Pneumonitis due to inhalation of food and vomit
Oxygen saturation in Arterial blood by Pulse oximetry

transform_flags: Provisional case_id SEQ (SPEC-NEURO-06-00231) — maintainer to confirm/reassign within the SPEC-NEURDV-06 bucket; cases:ingest --reseq finalises it.

Primary specialty_tag set to NEURO (schema specialty_tags enum does not include a neurodevelopmental code; NEURO is the closest allowed value for microcephaly/status epilepticus); tags also include EMG and RESP for the acute emergency + aspiration and PAEDS for the paediatric patient. case_id uses SPEC-NEURO-06; reviewer may prefer EMG as primary.

Difficulty tier set to multi_morbidity_complex (06); the case also strongly exhibits communication_barrier (07, non-verbal patient) and atypical_presentation_high_risk (04) features — reviewer may re-classify. Classification lives in case_metadata, not the id.

Telehealth reprojection applied: the source clinician administered buccal midazolam 10mg, performed oropharyngeal suctioning and positioned the patient. These in-person actions were reprojected to recognition + 000 escalation + carer-deliverable first aid; the AI Doctor administers nothing.

PAEDIATRIC NO-DOSE HARD LIMIT: the source states 'buccal Midazolam 10mg'. This dose was NOT carried into 12.medications.dose_route_frequency because the patient is under 18. Management defers to the patient's own prescribed PRN per his individualised seizure plan and flags for in-person/specialist review. Clinician to confirm the individualised rescue dose.
AMT code for midazolam deferred (not confidently known) rather than fabricated — omitted from 12.medications.amt_snomed_code per the code-honesty rule; terminology step to supply.

Clinician-measured vitals (BP 110/65, HR 125, RR 26, SpO2 92% room air, Temp 37.8, Wt 35kg) and auscultation findings were sealed into 11 (physical_sign/investigation_result) and 10.telehealth_limits, NOT surfaced in 01; carer-observable proxies (fast/noisy/dusky breathing, wet cough, drowsiness, twitching) were placed in 01.objective_data_offered as caregiver_reported/video_observable, verified:false.

De-anchoring applied: the KNOWN congenital baseline (microcephaly, spastic quadriplegia, developmental delay, epilepsy, tube feeding, non-verbal) is carried as patient/carer-stated history in 01; the NEW acute diagnosis (focal status epilepticus + aspiration) is sealed in 10-13.
Informant is a paid support worker, which is not a value in paediatric_context.informant enum; the field was omitted and the caregiver role captured in narrative and contextual_modifiers instead.

english_proficiency set to interpreter_required as a proxy for the non-verbal/communication-barrier status (patient cannot self-report); reviewer may prefer a dedicated communication-barrier representation.

ingest.hashing: SHA-256 over canonical (JSON 2-space + trailing newline) bytes of each split node
